mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization gains, creating short-term positive momentum that pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat following positive INDPRO revisions in > 60% of cases, or autocorrelation of returns dominates the signal.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation and restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and increases discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly translates to increased corporate earnings capacity, establishing a positive trend in equity valuations that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows, or returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and consumer spending power, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling retail sales signals unwanted inventory accumulation, prompting corporate earnings downgrades and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% or insignificant predictive regression coefficients.
